title: Nervilia mackinnonii
taxonomy: Nervilia mackinnonii is classified in the kingdom Plantae, specifically within the family Orchidaceae. This epiphytic orchid belongs to the genus Nervilia, characterized by its unique, flat leaves. Further classification places Nervilia mackinnonii within the tribe Coelogyneae, subfamily Spiranthoideae. This orchid is native to the Indian subcontinent and Southeast Asia, where it is often found growing on trees. Its classification reflects its evolutionary adaptations to its epiphytic habitat.

intro: Nervilia mackinnonii, commonly known as the Indian Nervilia or Mackinnon's Nervilia, is a species of orchid native to the Himalayas. Traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine, this plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antidiabetic properties, making it a useful treatment for various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and diabetes. The bioactive constituents of Nervilia mackinnonii include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Nervilia mackinnonii include decoctions, infusions, and tablets, which are used to treat a range of health issues.
